stimulus: Layering thin sheets of gold leaf over carved wooden panels, Tadahiko Nakamura’s 1991 sculpture Sunlit Pavilion draws on the tradition of kirikane, a Japanese decorative practice ______blank by the application of finely cut metal leaf to depict shimmering, otherworldly surfaces.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of verb forms within a sentence. The nonfinite past participle "distinguished" is correctly used within a supplementary element that modifies the noun phrase "a Japanese decorative practice," defining kirikane and explaining one of its identifying traits.

Choice B is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Using the finite present-tense verb "is distinguished" creates a second main clause, and the two main clauses can’t be joined this way by only the comma before "a Japanese." Choice C is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Using the finite present-perfect verb "has been distinguished" creates a second main clause. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Using the finite future-tense verb "will be distinguished" creates a second main clause.
